items.title: #NSTviral: Elephants stop traffic on Gerik-Jeli highway during Hari Raya rush [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A group of about five wild elephants was seen along the Gerik–Jeli Highway, a key route linking Perak and Kelantan through forested terrain. The animals brought traffic to a near standstill as motorists headed home to celebrate Hari Raya. A TikTok video has gone viral showing a tense encounter between motorists and the elephants along the Gerik–Jeli road during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri exodus.

items.title: Turkiye detains opposition journalist over 'false information'
items.summary: ISTANBUL: A journalist from a Turkish newspaper critical of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government was placed in pre-trial detention Sunday for "spreading false information", his publication said. Ismail Ari, a journalist for left-wing newspaper Birgun, was arrested on Saturday evening in the northern province of Tokat, which he was visiting to celebrate the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan, his paper said. "I was arrested because of a three-month-old video," Ari said in a statement sent by his lawyers, without giving details.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim's vision for Malaysia
items.summary: Sultan Ibrahim was born on Nov 22, 1958 and he turns 68 this year. Sultan Ibrahim was crowned the Sultan of Johor in a ceremony at Istana Besar on March 23, 2015, the first coronation in Johor in 55 years. Johor observes the sultan's birthday on March 23 each year.

items.title: Scenes from ‘Money Heist’? Not quite — just bolder, more sophisticated thieves as authorities race to keep up
items.summary: Unlike a cinematic heist, these criminals are not after money bags — their real prize is the highly sought-after copper inside power cables running along Klang Valley’s sprawling 207.5km-long mass rapid transit network. “What we have noticed is that since last year, they have already started attacking the power cables that power the trains,” Prasarana Group chief health, safety, security, environment and sustainable development officer Idzqandar Abu Bakar told Malay Mail in a recent interview. Prasarana Group chief health, safety, security, environment and sustainable development officer Idzqandar Abu Bakar speaking to Malay Mail during an interview recently.

items.title: Slovenia PM claims election win as results show neck and neck finish
items.summary: LJUBLJANA: Slovenia's incumbent liberal Prime Minister Robert Golob on Sunday claimed victory in parliamentary elections, even as official results showed a neck-and-neck finish with the conservatives of Donald Trump admirer Janez Jansa. The race was expected to be tight, and foreign interference claims shook the campaign, with authorities probing whether an Israeli company was behind secretly recorded videos suggesting alleged graft in Golob's government. The results put Golob's party on 29 seats compared to 28 for Jansa, in the 90-seat parliament.

items.title: N. Korea's Kim Jong Un reappointed as president of state affairs
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea's rubber-stamp legislature has re-elected Kim Jong Un as president of state affairs, state media reported Monday. Kim's reappointment as head of the authoritarian nation's highest policy-making and governing body, the State Affairs Commission, was announced by the state news agency, KCNA. "The Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK reelected Comrade Kim Jong Un as President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea at the First Session, the first state affairs activity of its 15th term, on March 22," the KCNA report said.

items.title: Staff absences soar at some US airports as ICE agents prepare to screen travellers
items.summary: NEW YORK: Absences among transportation security workers this weekend reached their highest since a partial government shutdown began five weeks ago, the Department of Homeland Security said on Sunday, as immigration enforcement agents prepared to fill in for them at some of the busiest US airports. DHS said on Sunday it would not publicly share details about the ICE deployment, in order to preserve operational security. Sources briefed on the matter said the current plan calls for deploying ICE agents to 14 locations, although that figure may change.

items.title: Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf energy and water after Trump ultimatum
items.summary: TEL AVIV/JERUSALEM/WASHINGTON: Iran said on Sunday it would strike the energy and water systems of its Gulf neighbours in retaliation if US President Donald Trump follows through with a threat delivered a day earlier to hit Iran's electricity grid in 48 hours, escalating the three-week-old war. Air raid sirens sounded across Israel from the early hours of Sunday, warning of incoming missiles from Iran, after scores of people were hurt overnight in two separate attacks in the southern Israeli towns of Arad and Dimona. The Israeli military said hours later that it was striking Teheran in response.

items.title: Heatwave warning for Padang Terap
items.summary: A Level 2 warning means the area is undergoing a heatwave. MetMalaysia said a Level 2 (heatwave) warning is issued when daily maximum temperatures range between 37°C and 40°C for at least three consecutive days. The Level 1 (alert) warning is issued when maximum temperatures reach between 35°C and 37°C for three straight days.

items.title: Top traffic cop hailed for aiding m-cyclist
items.summary: Netizens have praised Kuala Lumpur's top traffic police officer for going above and beyond his duties to help a motorcyclist involved in an accident. According to a post on the Kuala Lumpur traffic police Facebook page, the victim sustained minor injuries. User Parum Niot said: "A responsible senior police officer with a heart, well done!"

items.title: Taiwan to get delayed US fighter jets this year
items.summary: TAIPEI: Deliveries of delayed F-16V fighter jets for Taiwan will begin this year with production at "full capacity", the island's defence ministry said after senior defence officials visited the United States. The United States in 2019 approved a US$8 billion sale of Lockheed Martin F-16 fighter jets to Taiwan, a deal that would take the island's F-16 fleet to more than 200 jets. Deliveries will begin this year, the ministry said, without elaborating.

items.title: Bangladesh rushes to secure US$2b loan amid energy crisis
items.summary: DHAKA: Bangladesh is pushing to secure loans of around US$2 billion from multilateral agencies for tackling energy security concerns amid soaring global fuel prices driven by the Mideast war. The government has already taken several measures to curb fuel consumption, including halting production at most fertiliser factories. The government may also approach the World Bank.

items.title: Firemen battling blazes at bookshop, forest in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Fire and Rescue Department is actively carrying out operations to extinguish a fire at a two-storey bookshop along Jalan Datuk Syeh Ahmad here, tonight. The department's Negri Sembilan senior operations commander Mohd Hanafi Wahid said the fire broke out about 9pm, with no casualties reported. "So far, personnel from the Seremban station, with assistance from the Seremban 2 station, are actively conducting firefighting operations," he said in a statement tonight.

items.title: NST Leader: Making our mark in pursuit of happiness
items.summary: Happiness comes from the sense that life matters and has connection beyond yourself. Happiness is a by-product of living well, not a direct goal. People who find work, love or growth meaningful often find happiness, a layered concept weaving pleasure, meaning, connection and peace.

items.title: Search for missing elderly diver continues in Pulau Tenggol
items.summary: DUNGUN: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for an elderly man, who went missing while diving in Pulau Tenggol waters last Friday, has yet to yield any results. "In addition, the operation was also assisted by the local diving community involving Tenggol Coral Beach Resort with 15 divers, Tenggol Dive Buddy Dungun (six divers) and Ocean Dream Dungun (six divers)," the statement said. The statement also said the search operation was focused around the last location where the victim was reported missing and the nearby waters.

items.title: Thailand's Anutin returns with nationalist push
items.summary: WEEKS before Thailand's general election in February, Anutin Charnvirakul stood at a rally in the capital Bangkok and declared that his Bhumjaithai Party should be the automatic choice for any patriotic Thai. "I promise to you all that I will safeguard Thailand with my life," the 59-year-old prime minister said. The stump speech captured Anutin's strategy of riding a wave of nationalism washing through Thailand in the wake of a fierce border conflict with Cambodia last year — a gamble that paid off.

items.title: Iran war escalates beyond Trump's control
items.summary: PRESIDENT Donald Trump ends the third week of the Iran war confronting a crisis that seems to be slipping out of his hands: Global energy prices are surging, the United States stands isolated from allies and more troops are preparing to deploy despite his promise the war would be only a "short excursion". A defensive Trump called other North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (Nato) countries "cowards" for refusing to help secure the Strait of Hormuz and insisted the campaign was unfolding according to plan. Trump could go all-in and intensify the US offensive, possibly even seizing Iran's oil hub on Kharg Island or deploying troops along Iran's coast to hunt for missile launchers.

items.title: Aidilfitri joy turns to sadness as three families lose home in fire
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: The festive joy of Aidilfitri turned into grief for three families when the house they shared in Kampung Gong Kijang, Bukit Tunggal, was destroyed in a fire this afternoon. Kuala Nerus fire station chief Azmi Omar said 16 firemen aboard four engines from his station as well as those from the Kuala Terengganu and Jalan Kota stations were deployed to fight the blaze. "We had to get assistance from the two other stations as we were also involved in fighting a 0.8ha forest fire in Bandar Baharu Kuala Nerus," he said.

items.title: Iran to completely close Hormuz if US targets power plants
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's military threatened on Sunday to completely shut down the strategic Strait of Hormuz, among other measures, if US President Donald Trump acts on threats to target the country's power plants. "If the United States' threats regarding Iran's power plants are carried out... the Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed, and it will not be reopened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt," the military's operational command Khatam Al-Anbiya said in a statement carried by state television. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump orders ICE agents to airports amid crippling budget standoff
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Immigration agents will be deployed in US airports beginning Monday, aiming to alleviate soaring congestion at security screenings amid a weeks-long budget standoff over President Donald Trump's mass deportation drive, officials said. Rather, the agents would help "wherever we can provide extra security," such as monitoring exits. However, DHS also oversees the Transportation Security Administration (TSA), the agency responsible for airport security screenings, meaning staff around the country have worked for weeks without pay.

items.title: 32 summonses issued in Johor over illegal racing, reckless driving in Johor
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Police issued 32 summonses for various offences during Operasi Samseng Jalanan along Lebuh Medini Utama here early this morning. District police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the location was identified as a hotspot for dangerous activities, including illegal racing and reckless driving. "During the operation, police inspected 45 cars involving 63 men and 14 women aged 18 to 50.

items.title: Cuba readies for possible US attack, but ready to talk
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A top Cuban diplomat said the communist-led island is making preparations in the event of a potential US attack, but insisted it has "no quarrel" with Washington, according to an interview that aired Sunday. And in fact it is preparing these days for the possibility of military aggression," Cuba's deputy foreign minister Carlos Fernandez de Cossio told NBC, adding, however, "we truly hope that it doesn't occur." "Cuba has no quarrel with the United States.

items.title: Aeon Bank launches campaign emphasising good deeds, community support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Bank (M) Bhd has launched its Ramadan and Syawal campaign, themed "Niat di Hati, Budi Terpateri", focusing on good intentions and thoughtful actions leading to meaningful outcomes. As part of its ongoing community engagement, the bank held its fourth edition of the Salam Prihatin initiative, which benefited 100 households and over 400 individuals from Perumahan Pantai Permai, Kuala Lumpur. In addition to community support, Aeon Bank has introduced features in its digital banking app to assist customers with financial management.

items.title: Facts about Strait of Hormuz war blockade
items.summary: - 24 security incidents - Since March 1, 2026, 24 commercial vessels, including 11 tankers, have been attacked or reported incidents in the Gulf, the Strait of Hormuz or the Gulf of Oman, according to the British naval maritime security agency UKMTO. - 8 sea workers killed - Since the conflict began, at least eight seafarers or dock workers have died in incidents in the region, according to the International Maritime Organization (IMO). - 95 percent shipping drop - The channel typically sees around 120 daily transits, according to shipping industry intelligence site Lloyd's List.

items.title: Today's news in pictures – Sunday, March 22, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? [[nid:1401913]] KUALA LUMPUR – Members of the public celebrate the second day of Hari Raya Aidilfitri by taking photos at Panorama Bukit Antarabangsa, dressed in their finest traditional Raya attire. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) reported that Padang Terap reached Level 2 (Heatwave) as of 4 pm today.

items.title: Japan could consider Hormuz minesweeping if ceasefire reached, minister says
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan could consider deploying its military for minesweeping in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital artery for global oil supplies, if a ceasefire is reached in the US-Israeli war on Iran, Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi said on Sunday. Japan's military actions are limited under its postwar pacifist constitution, but 2015 security legislation allows Japan to use its Self-Defense Forces overseas if an attack, including on a close security partner, threatens Japan's survival and no other means are available to address it. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Japan's Kyodo news agency on Friday that he had spoken to Motegi about potentially letting Japanese-related vessels pass through the strait.

items.title: Rethinking water at home: Why Malaysia’s taps may not always run as smoothly as we think
items.summary: UALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Malaysia is used to treating water shortages as temporary disruptions. What is ‘water bankruptcy’? The UNU-INWEH uses “water bankruptcy” to describe a stage beyond the usual language of “water stress” or a “water crisis”.

items.title: Express bus crashes in Kinabatangan, all 40 on board escape uninjured
items.summary: KINABATANGAN: A total of 39 express bus passengers faced a harrowing ordeal when the bus they were traveling in veered off the road and crashed on its side near Kampung Paris 2, Jalan Sandakan-Lahad Datu, here today. District police chief Superintendent D. Ravi said the incident occurred when the bus driver reportedly swerved to avoid a motorcycle which had suddenly exited a junction on the left side of the road. However, Ravi said the driver and his 39 passengers were left unscathed.

items.title: Bersatu Youth wing urges probe after fireworks stall fire in Penang
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Bersatu Nibong Tebal Youth wing has urged police to clarify whether permits were issued for fireworks sales after a stall selling the items caught fire in Taman Pekaka earlier today. "We also want clear guidelines on permit issuance, including restrictions on sales in high-risk areas such as Ramadan bazaars, petrol stations and public transport hubs. They also called for clear and comprehensive guidelines on permit issuance, including restrictions on sales in high-risk areas such as Ramadan bazaars, petrol stations and public transport hubs.

items.title: Traffic snarls hit key highways as accidents worsen congestion nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Traffic on major highways is congested this evening following increased traffic and accidents at several locations. “Several accidents were also reported which contributed to congestion such as at Hutan Kampung Toll Plaza from Jitra and Tanjung Malim to Lembah Beringin,” he said when contacted today. He said on NSE southbound, traffic was congested from Ainsdale to Senawang and from Simpang Renggam to Sedenak.

items.title: Iran proposes to form regional security structure, reiterates conditions to end war
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Saturday proposed to form a security structure comprising regional countries to ensure peace, security and stability in West Asia without foreign interference, reiterating conditions to end the ongoing war, reported Xinhua. He made the remarks in a phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, in which the two sides discussed bilateral ties as well as the latest regional and international developments in the wake of the US and Israeli attacks against Iran, according to Iran's official news agency IRNA. He strongly condemned the United States and Israel for killing Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, high-ranking military commanders and civilians, as well as targeting the country's public infrastructure.

items.title: Stop spreading fake news, police say as probe into fatal Tanah Merah crash continues
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 22 — Police are still conducting a comprehensive investigation into a crash involving a Road Transport Department (JPJ) vehicle and a car at KM85.5 of the Kota Bharu-Machang-Jeli road in Tanah Merah, on Friday. Tanah Merah District Police chief Supt Mohd Haki Hasbullah said the investigation covered various aspects, including witness statements, forensic analysis and technical examinations at the scene. Elaborating further, Mohd Haki said claims that police had made conclusions without basis were untrue.

items.title: Sandakan crash kills mother, daughter after motorcycle collides with car
items.summary: SANDAKAN, March 22 — A mother and daughter were killed when the motorcycle they were on collided with a car along Jalan Batu 23, Jalan Labuk here yesterday. Sandakan district police chief ACP George Abd Rakman said the daughter, aged seven, died at the scene at 4pm while the mother, 28, died while being treated at Duchess of Kent Hospital here. “The motorcyclist and car driver were also injured and were treated at Duchess of Kent Hospita.

items.title: Heavy traffic on Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway in both directions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic along the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway was slow-moving in both eastbound and westbound directions as of 5.55pm today. Congestion was observed on the eastbound stretch from Bukit Tinggi to Lentang (Km 51.1), as well as from Lentang (Km 57.1) to Bentong. Westbound traffic also built up from Bentong to Bukit Tinggi, and further from Genting Sempah to the Gombak toll plaza.

items.title: MOH eyes RM485,000 upgrade for century-old trauma training facility in Sungai Buloh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is studying a proposal to upgrade the National Clinical Integration and Trauma Life Support Malaysia (TLSM) Training Centre at Bukit Ilmu, Hospital Sungai Buloh, which is more than 100 years old. He said the Sungai Buloh Hospital carries a major responsibility as a national referral centre and serves as a backbone for the northern Selangor corridor, including the Kuala Selangor parliamentary constituency. Meanwhile, Sungai Buloh Hospital said in an earlier Facebook post that the training centre serves as a key hub in producing highly skilled healthcare professionals in emergency and trauma care.

items.title: NSE traffic slows as accidents trigger congestion on major highways
items.summary: An accident was also reported at Km 410 northbound from Bukit Tagar to Lembah Beringin, causing further slowdowns. Another accident occurred at Km 105.4 northbound between Sungai Petani Selatan and Sungai Petani Utara, with the emergency lane obstructed. Further northbound slowdowns were reported from Ipoh Utara to Menora, Bukit Berapit to Changkat Jering, Bukit Merah to Alor Pongsu, as well as between Jawi and Bandar Cassia, Permatang Pauh to Sungai Petani Utara and Gurun to the Gurun rest area.

items.title: Aidilfitri interrupted as bridge collapse plunges Raya visitors into sea at Pulau Gaya
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 22 — A bridge in the water village of Kampung Selamat, Lok Urai, Pulau Gaya, collapsed, sending several Hari Raya visitors into the sea. In a statement, the office of the Tanjung Aru assemblyman said it received news of the incident on the first day of Hari Raya Aidilfitri yesterday. “While our friends were joyfully celebrating Aidilfitri after a month of fasting, a bridge in Kampung Selamat, Lok Urai, Pulau Gaya reportedly collapsed, causing several residents to fall into the sea.

items.title: Sultan Abdullah and Pahang royals join Aidilfitri celebration hosted by MB Wan Rosdy
items.summary: TEMERLOH, March 22 — About 20,000 people attended the Aidilfitri open house hosted by Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail at Dewan Sri Lanchang here today. The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, Tengku Ampuan Pahang Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah and Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, along with other members of the royal family, graced the event. Also present were members of the Pahang State Executive Council.

items.title: Melaka exco member Rais Yasin warns public against open burning amid hot, dry season to prevent fire risk
items.summary: MELAKA, March 22 — The public is advised to avoid open burning during this hot and dry season, said the Melaka Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin. He said it was to avoid triggering a fire risk that could spread quickly. “Therefore, I hope that all the people in Melaka can take precautionary measures and avoid open burning,” he said when met by reporters at his open house event in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri at the Hang Jebat Stadium in Krubong here today.

items.title: National Anti‑Drugs Agency reports 81pc recovery rate among 26,852 clients nationwide
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 22 — A total of 21,794 clients who underwent community-based treatment and rehabilitation programmes, nationwide, achieved permanent recovery status last year. National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said that the recovery rate reached 81 per cent, out of the total 26,852 clients evaluated. Elaborating further, Ruslin said that last year, a total of 1,500 drug addicts voluntarily reported to the Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres (Puspen) to undergo treatment and rehabilitation programmes.

items.title: Human Resources minister hails young Malaysians’ international success at Bangkok Innovation Awards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Four Malaysian students excelled at the Bangkok Innovation Awards, winning two gold medals and second place in the Engineering category for primary schools. The four students are Ilaneyan Yuvarajan and M. Theerchanna from Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) KO Sarangapany, as well as Ilanarun Yuvarajan and M. Elamaaran from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Sultan Badlishah. Ramanan, who is also the Member of Parliament for Sungai Buloh, expressed appreciation to the students’ parents who were willing to sacrifice their time to travel to Bangkok to give moral support.

items.title: Pahang police: Contractor transferred funds 16 times before realising ‘marinasand’ investment was a scam
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim, a 34-year-old man, saw an investment advertisement on Google on February 18 through a website known as “marinasand”. “The victim then registered and participated in the investment, which promised returns of 10 per cent on the total investment,” he said in a statement today. He said the victim had made 16 money transfer transactions using personal savings as well as company funds into five different bank accounts from February 18 to March 19, 2026.

items.title: Crowds queue from dawn as Sultan Ibrahim hosts Aidilfitri open house in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 22 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, graced the Aidilfitri open house at Istana Besar here today. The King was accompanied by Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia; the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail; his consort Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Bustamam; and Their Royal Highnesses’ children. Queen Raja Zarith Sofiah shares handshakes and smiles with the crowd at the Johor palace Aidilfitri open house today.

items.title: Malaysia’s energy supply remains stable despite West Asia tensions, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — The Federal Government has given an assurance that the country’s energy supply remains stable despite global uncertainties following conflicts in West Asia, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. “So far, the country’s energy supply remains at a stable level due to early planning and diversification of supply sources. “The government, through relevant ministries, is closely monitoring water supply together with water providers in each state to ensure sufficient supply.

items.title: Sarawak studies tougher rabies control as cat infections pose growing challenge
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — The Sarawak state government is studying the best approaches to strengthen efforts in controlling rabies, particularly infections among cats, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. He said discussions have been held with the Sarawak State Health Department and the Department of Veterinary Services Sarawak to develop an integrated action plan. Dr Sim, who is also Sarawak’s Minister for Public Health, Housing and Local Government, reminded the public not to take the rabies threat lightly and to always take preventive measures, including ensuring their pets receive regular vaccinations.

items.title: Kelantan MB backs cost-saving Raya open houses, says use personal funds over public money
items.summary: PASIR MAS, March 22 — Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud expressed support for a cost-saving approach in organising open house events, including using personal funds without relying on government allocations. He said the approach of using one’s own expenses is in line with the government’s call for celebrations to be held in moderation. “For the state’s economy, open houses can sometimes be held, so we organise this using personal funds, while government events depend on the situation,” he said when met by reporters at his open house in Meranti here today, which was attended by about 5,000 guests.

items.title: Penang CM submits report for Anthony Loke on state quit rent hike, vows media silence
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 22 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow will prepare a report to be submitted to DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke regarding the implementation of new quit rent (land tax) rates, which came into effect on Jan 1 in the state. “I want to express my appreciation to the DAP secretary-general for arranging a meeting involving all three parties (Chow, Loke and former Penang CM Lim Guan Eng) to discuss the issue of the increase in quit rent rates in Penang, he said in a statement today. The revision of quit rent rates involves nearly 370,000 land titles throughout Penang, covering various land categories and uses, including residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural and other special categories.

items.title: Why Malaysia still feels global oil shocks despite producing its own, Anwar explains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia has not been spared from the recent surge in global oil prices following the conflict in West Asia, despite being an oil producing nation. He said the disruption at the Strait of Hormuz — the world’s main oil shipping route — has limited global supply and pushed prices higher. Nearly half of Malaysia’s oil supply passes through the strait, he noted.

items.title: Juvenile whale found dead at Tanjung Aru Beach in KK days after release from Sutera Harbour jetty
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 22 — A juvenile whale previously spotted at the Sutera Harbour Resort jetty and released back into the sea was found dead at Tanjung Aru Beach near Shangri-La Tanjung Aru Resort, on Saturday. According to Info X, the carcass of the marine mammal was discovered stranded between rocks along the beach at about noon. Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel on duty at the beach made the discovery.

items.title: Leaks, theft and ageing pipes: Fadillah vows bold push to fix Malaysia’s water woes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — The issue of non-revenue water (NRW), which remains high in several states, including pipe leaks, water theft and ageing infrastructure, significantly impacts the efficiency of the country’s water supply system. “This includes investment in smart technology, holistic water resource management, and inter-agency cooperation to ensure the country’s water security is always guaranteed,” he said in a Facebook post in conjunction with World Water Day (WWD) celebrations today. Therefore, he said the Water Sector Transformation Roadmap 2040 (AIR 2040) also serves as Malaysia’s long-term plan to improve the country’s water management, ensure sufficient clean water supply, and make the water sector more modern and sustainable.

items.title: Sarawak firefighters extricate victim as Miri traffic light crash claims life and injures two others
items.summary: SIBU, March 22 — A woman was killed while two others were injured in a road accident involving a car and a four-wheel-drive (4WD) vehicle at the Vista Perdana traffic lights in Miri early this morning. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the 23-year-old woman was pronounced dead at the scene by Ministry of Health (MOH) personnel. “Firefighters extricated the woman from a Perodua Bezza.

items.title: Three family members killed in Pulau Tioman motorcycle crash laid to rest in Muar cemetery
items.summary: MUAR, March 22 — Three family members, who were killed in a motorcycle accident on Jalan Tekek-Juara, Pulau Tioman, Pahang, last Friday, were laid to rest at the Batu 6 Muslim Cemetery, Bakri, at 12.15am today. The remains of Mohd Sabri Ja’afar, 42; his wife, Siti Aisyah Suhaime, 32; and their five-year-old son, Muhammad Yusuf Abdurrahman Mohd Sabri, arrived at Surau Ar-Rahman, Bakri, at about 10.45pm, before funeral prayers were performed by the congregation and family members. Rompin police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi was reported to have said that the incident caused Mohd Sabri, who was a customer service officer at the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia, along with his wife and son, to die at the scene.

items.title: Maritime agency deploys boat and sets up base in Kuala Dungun after diver disappears off Pulau Tenggol
items.summary: Kemaman Maritime Zone director Maritime Commander Abdul Halim Hamzah said the incident occurred at 4.30pm when Tam Peng Hoong, 66, was diving in the area with his son. He said the Maritime Rescue Sub Centre (MRSC) Kuantan, Pahang, received a report on the incident this afternoon, and a search and rescue (SAR) operation was activated at 2.10pm, nine minutes after the report was received. The SAR operation will resume Sunday morning,” he said.

items.title: Second teenage boy found drowned at Sungai Plan Lot waterfall in Bintulu after swimming with friends
items.summary: SIBU, March 22 — Another teenage boy who was reported missing while swimming with friends at the Sungai Plan Lot waterfall, Jalan Permata, Kidurong, Bintulu, yesterday, was found drowned late last night. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said that the victim’s body, identified as Darren Dakun Paruscrum, 12, was found by members of the public at 11.19pm, about five metres from the riverbank. The body was later handed over to the police for further action,” Sarawak JBPM said in a statement.

items.title: Police issue 19 traffic summonses during two-night crime prevention operation in Bau
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — A total of 48 vehicles were inspected and 19 traffic summonses issued during a crime prevention operation conducted over two nights from March 20 to 21. The integrated operation, known as Op Bersepadu Pencegahan Jenayah, was carried out from 10pm on March 20 until 3am the following day, covering Bau town, the residential areas in Bau and Jambusan, and a roadblock at KM2 on Jalan Bau–Serikin. According to the Bau District Police Headquarters (IPD Bau) in a Facebook post, 48 vehicles and 60 individuals were checked during the operation.

items.title: Indonesian consul highlights growing travel and economic ties with Sarawak amid undocumented worker concerns
items.summary: KUCHING, March 22 — Increasing cross-border movement between Sarawak and Indonesia is driving economic and social exchange, but also bringing persistent challenges such as undocumented migration, says Indonesia’s Consul General in Kuching Abdullah Zulkifli. He said the recent holiday period in Indonesia from March 18 to 24 saw many Indonesians travelling long distances to Sarawak, including some who journeyed by land for over 20 hours via Pontianak before reaching Kuching. Despite these positive developments, Abdullah acknowledged ongoing challenges involving Indonesian migrant workers in Sarawak, particularly those entering or working without proper documentation.

items.title: Teen girl allegedly raped by new acquaintance in Maran, suspect arrested
items.summary: Maran district police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said a report was lodged by the victim’s mother regarding the incident. According to the report, the alleged incident took place in early March at a house in Maran when the victim went out with the suspect. Following the report, a team of officers from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Maran district police headquarters arrested the suspect on Friday for further investigation He said police are conducting further investigations under Section 376 of the Penal Code to gather evidence related to the case.

items.title: Kelantan police tighten border watch, reminding Hari Raya travellers to cross legally at authorised ICQS checkpoints
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 22 — Police have advised locals living along Sungai Golok along the Malaysia-Thailand border to use legal routes if they wish to visit relatives in conjunction with Aidilfitri. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said police are looking into the matter seriously due to the possibility of an increase in illegal cross-border movement, especially as water levels in Sungai Golok have become increasingly shallow. Earlier, Rantau Panjang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) deputy commander Bakri Sulaiman said about 50,000 travellers are expected to use the Rantau Panjang ICQS complex within three days before and three days after Hari Raya Aidilfitri.

items.title: Scorching days ahead as MetMalaysia flags heatwave in Kedah, with Penang, Perak and Negeri Sembilan on alert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 22 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) today issued a Level 2 (heatwave) warning for Padang Terap, Kedah. MetMalaysia, in a statement issued at 6am today, said that 12 areas in Peninsular Malaysia are at Level 1 (alert), involving Perlis and Kedah (Baling, Sik, Pendang, Pokok Sena, Kubang Pasu, Langkawi Island, Kuala Muda and Bandar Baharu). According to the agency, the heatwave level refers to a daily maximum temperature of 37 to 40 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while the alert level refers to a daily maximum temperature of 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Regent of Johor throws open palace doors, Singapore ministers cross Causeway for Hari Raya celebration
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 22 — Two Singapore Cabinet ministers attended a Hari Raya Aidilfitri Open House hosted by the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, at Istana Bukit Serene on Saturday, reaffirming strong cross-border ties and cooperation. Social and Family Development Minister Masagos Zulkifli and Education Minister Desmond Lee, together with their spouses, conveyed their Hari Raya greetings to His Royal Highness the Tunku Mahkota of Johor and Her Highness Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda of Johor. “This visit continues the annual tradition of Singapore’s leaders attending the Johor royal family’s Hari Raya Open House,” the statement read.
